title: Strong SECURITY/architectural comments throughout
description:
Multiple files demonstrate excellent commenting practice worth defending:

1. lora_l2.c:66-76 (generate_eui64): Explains SHA-256 rationale with MCU-specific hwid layout examples (STM32 lot/wafer, ESP32 MAC length, nRF52). Justifies hashing rather than truncating.

2. lichen_l2.c:79-87 (lichen_iface): Documents thread-safety invariant with issue reference (project-LICHEN-ybal.4), explains why pointer is never cleared, ties to Zephyr's interface model.

3. lichen_l2.c:222-242 (lichen_l2_enable): Documents lock ordering to prevent deadlock, explains SECURITY risk if cleanup moves outside mutex scope.

4. ipv6_addr.c:51-56, ipv6_addr.h:73-75 (pubkey_to_iid): SECURITY prefix explains why SHA-256 hash rather than raw pubkey bytes, references RFC 7343 and Python implementation.

These comments capture bus-factor knowledge that would otherwise be lost. If someone suggests 'simplifying' by removing them, push back.
close_reason: Positive review findings - worth defending, not fixing
